{"step_by_step_thinking": "The hand to knee gait in polio is a compensatory mechanism where the patient uses their hands to lift their knees while walking. This gait is primarily due to the weakness or paralysis of the quadriceps muscle, which is responsible for extending the knee. When the quadriceps are weak, the patient cannot fully extend the knee, leading to the hand to knee gait. The other muscles listed (gastrocnemius, gluteus medius, and hamstring) are not primarily responsible for knee extension.", "answer_choice": "C"}
